substrate: Fork-A `ledger` repo + off-disk wire CONTRACT.md; held-out accept.py (anti-idiomatic contract, not inferable from pristine code)
model: claude/sonnet throughout all hops (isolates residue value from any cross-model confound)
design: 3-hop chain architect->to_wire->from_wire. Two PHYSICALLY SEPARATE worktrees (wt-bridge / wt-nobridge) so the contract cannot leak between arms; pristine-asserted before every trial.
status: SCREEN — N>=8 confirm pre-registered (see REPORT Table 1) and pending

pointers+derived.transport: architect writes the contract to DERIVED.md; downstream receives repo://DERIVED.md

pointers-only.transport: downstream receives only repo://ledger/account.py (pristine); must infer the wire format
pointers-only.failure_mode: idiomatic keys / ISO-string ts instead of the contract's acct/cents/ts epoch-int

cold.note: Fork-A cross-model no-bridge (Exp. 2), included for the three-arm comparison

verdict: GO at screen level: in the regime where the reasoning is genuinely irreducible (a constraint invisible in the final code), the derived residue is NECESSARY (3/3 vs 0/3) and FREE (cheaper than the failing null). The panel's 're-derives cheaply' failure mode does NOT fire for a capable same-model receiver here. Confirm at N>=8 before promoting beyond a strong screen.
usd_weighting_note: Residue is +18% raw channel tokens but -18% net USD vs pointers-only (the null arm explores then fails). Raw-token and USD deltas point opposite ways (REPORT Fig 9).
validity: Three earlier screen attempts were VOID and discarded: (1) reset reverted only ledger/, leaving contract-encoding tests; (2) shared-worktree side channels; (3) wt-nobridge lacked the sonnet runner -> null no-op'd (turns/cost/wall=0). Each caught by checking the numbers were physically possible. This result is the post-fix isolated run.
